item.title:
A SMALL PRICE FOR PEACE
“Temporary oil price hikes are a small price for U.S. and world security. Prices will drop fast once Iran’s nuclear threat is gone. Only fools think otherwise.” — President DJT

item.title: 🚨 IRAQI OIL PRODUCTION FROM SOUTHERN OILFIELDS FALLS TO 1.3 MILLION BPD FROM AROUND 4.3 MILLION BPD - THREE SOURCES SAY
item.description: 🚨 IRAQI OIL PRODUCTION FROM SOUTHERN OILFIELDS FALLS TO 1.3 MILLION BPD FROM AROUND 4.3 MILLION BPD - THREE SOURCES SAY

item.title:
U.S. ENERGY CHIEF: GAS PRICE SPIKE WILL BE SHORT-LIVED
U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright said rising gasoline prices linked to the Iran conflict should ease soon.
Speaking on CNN and Fox News, Wright said the surge is driven mainly by uncertainty around the conflict and is likely to last weeks, not months. He expects energy shipments to continue flowing through the Strait of Hormuz.
The comments follow similar remarks from President Donald Trump, who said fuel prices should fall quickly.
Wright also noted the U.S. has been refilling the Strategic Petroleum Reserve and stressed that Iran’s oil and gas infrastructure is not a target of U.S. military strikes.
item.description:
U.S. ENERGY CHIEF: GAS PRICE SPIKE WILL BE SHORT-LIVED

U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright said rising gasoline prices linked to the Iran conflict should ease soon.

Speaking on CNN and Fox News, Wright said the surge is driven mainly by uncertainty around the conflict and is likely to last weeks, not months. He expects energy shipments to continue flowing through the Strait of Hormuz.

The comments follow similar remarks from President Donald Trump, who said fuel prices should fall quickly.

Wright also noted the U.S. has been refilling the Strategic Petroleum Reserve and stressed that Iran’s oil and gas infrastructure is not a target of U.S. military strikes.

item.title:
TRUMP: IRAN’S NEXT SUPREME LEADER MUST HAVE U.S. APPROVAL
President Donald Trump said Iran’s next supreme leader “won’t last long” without U.S. approval.
In an interview with ABC News, Trump said the new leader “is going to have to get approval from us,” warning that without it, the leader would not remain in power.
Trump added his goal is to prevent Iran from becoming a nuclear power and avoid repeated crises in the future, saying he does not want the U.S. to face the same threat again in five or ten years.
item.description:
TRUMP: IRAN’S NEXT SUPREME LEADER MUST HAVE U.S. APPROVAL

President Donald Trump said Iran’s next supreme leader “won’t last long” without U.S. approval.

In an interview with ABC News, Trump said the new leader “is going to have to get approval from us,” warning that without it, the leader would not remain in power.

Trump added his goal is to prevent Iran from becoming a nuclear power and avoid repeated crises in the future, saying he does not want the U.S. to face the same threat again in five or ten years.

item.description: The disappearance of the mother of TV presenter Savannah Guthrie captivated the US, but police have not made any arrests.

item.title:
DIESEL PRICES HIT HIGHEST LEVEL IN OVER THREE YEARS
Average diesel prices have climbed to $4.59 per gallon, according to GasBuddy data. This surpasses the previous peak from September 2023 and marks the highest level since February 8, 2023 — 1,129 days ago.
item.description:
DIESEL PRICES HIT HIGHEST LEVEL IN OVER THREE YEARS

Average diesel prices have climbed to $4.59 per gallon, according to GasBuddy data. This surpasses the previous peak from September 2023 and marks the highest level since February 8, 2023 — 1,129 days ago.

item.title: Explosion at US embassy in Oslo may have been terrorism, Norway police say
item.description: Police say they are investigating other motives after the blast caused minor damage to the embassy building.

item.title: Tehran residents warned of acid rain after oil storage attack
item.description: Iran’s Red Crescent Society tells locals to avoid exposure as capital is engulfed in smoke

item.title: UK opens charter flights booking portal for Britons in Dubai
item.description: It comes after thousands of flights had been cancelled in the Middle East in the past week.
